title: Network Automation Lab 004: Ansible and Netmiko
description: Submission template for the existing Network Automation Lab 004 covering Ansible structure, inventory awareness, Netmiko connection logic, and safe network automation evidence.

required_evidence.label: Automation target
required_evidence.description: Identify the lab target, such as router, switch, inventory group, or test host. Do not submit credentials.

required_evidence.label: Ansible project structure
required_evidence.description: Describe or show the Ansible files used, such as ansible.cfg, inventory, playbook, roles, or vault placeholders. Do not include secrets.

required_evidence.label: Netmiko workflow
required_evidence.description: Describe the Netmiko script purpose, target, command intent, and expected output. Do not include usernames, passwords, tokens, or private keys.

required_evidence.label: Safety check
required_evidence.description: Show how you limited risk before running automation, such as read-only commands, inventory scope, check mode, backup first, or test-only target.

required_evidence.label: Execution or dry-run evidence
required_evidence.description: Provide output or observations from a safe run, dry run, syntax check, or read-only automation validation.

required_evidence.label: Validation
required_evidence.description: Explain how you proved the automation result was correct.

required_evidence.label: Rollback or recovery
required_evidence.description: Explain how you would recover from a bad automation result or restore from backup.

required_evidence.label: Professional summary
required_evidence.description: Write a short network automation handoff summary.

completion_signals: student provides execution or dry-run evidence
student includes rollback or recovery thinking
safety_rules: Do not submit router passwords, API keys, vault passwords, private keys, tokens, or session cookies.
Use read-only commands before configuration changes.
Back up network configuration before changing network devices.
Limit automation scope to the approved lab target.
Do not push configuration changes unless the lab explicitly requires it.
